channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: The full Kingdom Hearts saga is coming to Switch 2, PS5 and Xbox Series X|S
item.content:encoded:
Square Enix has announced that the full Kingdom Hearts saga so far is being upgraded and ported to PS5, Xbox Series X|S and Nintendo Switch 2, launching on 8th October 2026.

As part of this, the KINGDOM HEARTS Collection [I~III] will be sold as an all-in-one bundle for people that want the whole lot,  but you can also buy each chapter separately. That’s KINGDOM HEARTS -HD 1.5+2.5 ReMIX-, KINGDOM HEARTS HD 2.8 Final Chapter Prologue and KINGDOM HEARTS III + Re Mind (DLC).

I know I’m not the first one to say this, but the way these remastered collections have been named over the years is beyond ridiculous.

The big news for Switch 2 buyers is that these will now be running natively on the console, where the Switch 1 releases were all ‘Cloud ver.’ and played remotely. That was Goofy at the time and it’s still pretty quackers, if you ask Donald me. A demo of Kingdom Hearts 3 is available today for Switch 2 featuring the start of the game through to Olympus (with save data that can carry across to the full game), and then jumping ahead to Toy Box World (where save data will not carry over)

Upgrade pricing is also available, with a 50% discount on digital purchases from last gen to Switch 2, PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. Switch 1 to Switch 2 will also be able to transfer save data, but this is a mysteriously absent feature on Xbox and PlayStation. I see no good reason why it shouldn’t be possible, given that there’s countless other games that have a save transfer between generations.

Square Enix also released a new teaser trailer for Kingdom Hearts IV, confirming that it will now also be coming to Switch 2.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/the-full-kingdom-hearts-saga-is-coming-to-switch-2-ps5-and-xbox-series-xs/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pikuniku 2 enters the third dimension in 2027
item.content:encoded:
The puzzle-exploration sequel Pikuniku 2 has been revealed, and this time its quirky adventure is in 3D.

Pikuniku 2 is coming to PC and Switch 2 in 2027

Pikuniku 2 starts with Piku and friends going off on a picnic, only for a sandwich to escape, a boat to get wrecked, and everyone to get split up across an archipelago. So it’s up to Piku to go and find everyone.

Along the way, Piku will meet a bunch of strange people, hear their stories – including stuff about giant fruit, evil robot frogs and an all-powerful potato. There’s also a new deep state conspiracy to unravel.

I thoroughly enjoyed the original Pikuniku, which launched some seven years ago. Miguel also loved it, and said in our Pikuniku review, “My favorite gaming experiences are the ones that elicit a pure and physical response from me. Whether it’s a horror game making me clench my cheeks or a story-driven game dropping my jaw and pulling a tear from my eye, those kinds of experiences are the ones that stick with me the most. With Pikuniku, I was smiling and laughing throughout the entire thing, and too few games get that kind of response out of me. Pikuniku is a treasure of a game, and I can’t wait to see more from this team.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/pikuniku-2-enters-the-third-dimension-in-2027/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nintendo Switch Sports Resort brings another 12 games to Wuhu Island
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo Switch Sports Resort has been revealed for, inviting you to Wuhu Island (not in a The Sims kind of way) to play 12 new and returning sports.

Switch Sports Resort is coming out on 22nd October exclusively for Nintendo Switch 2.

Using the Joy-Con 2 for motion controls, Switch Sports Resort will feature the following sports:

  • Boxing
  • Table Tennis
  • Archery
  • Tennis
  • Volleyball
  • Bowling
  • Basketball
  • Golf
  • Thumb Wrestling
  • Skateboarding
  • Power Cruising
  • Prop Plane

And you can also warm up with Jump Rope.

A bunch of these are returning from the last Switch Sports. Of the new ones, there’s some inventive new controller uses, such as needing mouse mode to play Skateboarding, or using Joy-Con as handlebars for the jet ski racing of Power Cruising.

You’ll be able to play once again as your Mii or a SportsMii, which has enhanced customisation options.

Online play will be included, with details on the player count for each game to be revealed at a later date.

Back with the last game, Jason said in our Switch Sports review, “Nintendo Switch Sports is a fun reminder of the Wii Sports craze, bringing back some classic sports and adding some new ones that make use of the Joy-Con and motion controls in more advanced ways. It’s all just a bit shallow, though, and difficult to wholeheartedly recommend unless you expect to play online a lot or constantly break it out for local head-to-head battles.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/nintendo-switch-sports-resort-brings-another-12-games-to-wuhu-island/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Orbitals brings its anime It Takes Two co-op action to Switch 2 in September
item.content:encoded:
Orbitals has a release date, the 90s anime riff on the beloved It Takes Two style co-op adventure set to launch exclusively for Switch 2 on 3rd September 2026.

The graphical style really shines through here, really capturing the look and feel of classic Japanese animation to go alongside the familiar looking split-screen co-op. The game sees explorers Maki and Omura setting out from their home to try and save it from an impending cosmic storm. They’ll be making use of separate abilities to support each others’ adventuring, using things like a grapple gun to pull out platforms, or cool lava with a water gun.

The game can also be played across multiple systems using GameShare, so you don’t have to play on TV or with a single Joy-Con.

Announced back at The Game Awards and then reappearing in a Partner Direct earlier this year, the game is in development by Tokyo-based indie team Shapefarm, but with assistance from animation house Studio Massket to craft its cutscenes. The game will have both Japanese and English audio dubs.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/orbitals-brings-its-anime-it-takes-two-co-op-action-to-switch-2-in-september/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
